current_conditions.site_name: SOQUE RIVER AT GA 197, NEAR CLARKESVILLE, GA

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.
reasoning: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.
No recent stocking signal is available.

generated_text.quick_read: The Soque River currently offers fair trout fishing conditions with stable flow and moderate weather. Evening of day 1 provides the best fishing window due to favorable light and pressure conditions.
generated_text.best_window: The evening of day 1 is the best window because the light is moderate and pressure conditions are neutral, encouraging trout to feed actively near runs, seams, and structure with a balanced nymph approach.
generated_text.current_conditions: USGS reports a flow of 64 cfs and gage height of 3.55 ft; water temperature and turbidity data are unavailable.
generated_text.weather_setup: Sky cover is low to moderate around 16%, pressure is 30.03 inHg with no strong trend, wind averages 5.3 mph from the west, and rain chance is low at 18%.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout favor comfortable feeding lanes near cover during higher light. Use clean drifts with smaller natural nymph patterns, focusing on runs, seams, and structure. No recent stocking reduces reliance on stocked patterns; balanced nymph tactics are recommended.
